metadata.title: Comprehensive Menstrual Pain Dataset
metadata.description: A detailed medical reference dataset covering all pain types, symptoms, precautions, and conditions related to menstruation

pain_types_and_body_parts.description: Cramping, throbbing or aching caused by uterine contractions driven by prostaglandins
Deep pelvic pressure or heaviness; may feel like something bearing down
Mittelschmerz-like ache if ovulation pain continues into period onset
Dull, continuous ache from uterine prostaglandins radiating to lumbar nerves
Muscle tension and stiffness between shoulder blades; less common than lumbar pain
Referred pain from pelvic congestion travelling upward through fascial lines
Hormonal-drop headache; throbbing behind forehead, often bilateral
Tension-type headache at temples triggered by oestrogen withdrawal
Full menstrual migraine with aura; nausea, light and sound sensitivity, visual disturbances
Stiffness and aching in cervical spine; worsened by tension headache
Aching in hip joints from pelvic inflammatory response and ligament laxity
Referred pain from uterine nerve pathways into inner and front thighs
Sciatic nerve irritation from pelvic congestion; shooting pain down back of leg
Joint ache due to prostaglandin-related inflammation; worsened by water retention
Cramping and oedema in lower legs from water retention and circulation changes
Cyclic mastalgia – tenderness, swelling and lumpiness from oestrogen and progesterone peaks
Hypersensitivity and soreness due to hormonal changes in oestrogen and progesterone
Costochondral tenderness or tightness; worsened by deep breathing during bloating
Nausea and cramping in upper GI from prostaglandin effect on smooth muscle
Diarrhoea, cramping and bloating from prostaglandins stimulating bowel motility
Rectal pressure and tenesmus (urge to defecate) from uterine proximity and pelvic congestion
Flu-like myalgia and fatigue from systemic prostaglandin and cytokine release
Inflammatory joint aches across multiple joints due to hormonal immune modulation
External pelvic pain and vulvodynia flares; burning or rawness worsened during flow

physical.name: Bloating
physical.description: Abdominal distension and fullness from water retention and GI motility changes

physical.name: Fatigue & Exhaustion
physical.description: Profound tiredness from blood loss, hormonal fluctuation and poor sleep quality

physical.name: Nausea & Vomiting
physical.description: Nausea from prostaglandins acting on GI tract; vomiting in severe dysmenorrhoea

physical.name: Diarrhoea
physical.description: Loose stools triggered by prostaglandin-induced bowel hypermotility

physical.name: Constipation
physical.description: Slowed bowel transit in luteal phase from elevated progesterone levels

physical.name: Dizziness / Lightheadedness
physical.description: Drop in blood pressure from blood loss and prostaglandin-induced vasodilation

physical.name: Fainting (Vasovagal)
physical.description: Vagal nerve response to intense pain causing transient loss of consciousness

physical.name: Hot Flushes / Sweating
physical.description: Hormonal thermoregulation disruption causing sudden heat surges and night sweats

physical.name: Chills
physical.description: Cold sensations and shivering linked to prostaglandin-induced temperature dysregulation

physical.name: Acne Breakouts
physical.description: Androgen surge before period triggers increased sebum production and pore blockage

physical.name: Water Retention / Oedema
physical.description: Fluid retention in limbs and face from oestrogen and aldosterone interaction

physical.name: Heavy Menstrual Bleeding
physical.description: Excessive flow over 80 ml per cycle requiring hourly pad changes; iron-depleting

physical.name: Passage of Blood Clots
physical.description: Large blood clots over 2.5 cm indicating heavy flow or possible fibroid shedding

physical.name: Insomnia / Poor Sleep
physical.description: Difficulty falling or staying asleep from pain, temperature dysregulation and cramps

emotional_psychological.name: Mood Swings
emotional_psychological.description: Rapid shifts between irritability, sadness and euphoria from oestrogen fluctuation

emotional_psychological.name: Anxiety
emotional_psychological.description: Heightened worry or panic linked to progesterone and GABA receptor interaction

emotional_psychological.name: Depression / Low Mood
emotional_psychological.description: Persistent low mood from serotonin drop associated with falling oestrogen levels

emotional_psychological.name: Irritability
emotional_psychological.description: Low frustration threshold and snapping at minor triggers from neurochemical changes

emotional_psychological.name: Brain Fog
emotional_psychological.description: Difficulty concentrating, poor short-term memory and mental fatigue from hormonal shifts

emotional_psychological.name: Tearfulness / Crying
emotional_psychological.description: Increased emotional sensitivity and tearfulness without a clear external trigger

emotional_psychological.name: Food Cravings
emotional_psychological.description: Strong cravings for carbohydrates, sugar and salt from serotonin and blood sugar dips

emotional_psychological.name: Low Energy Levels
emotional_psychological.description: Reduced sexual interest from fatigue, pain and hormonal low point during menstruation

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Apply 40–42°C pad to lower abdomen or back for 20 minutes every 1–2 hours
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Avoid on broken skin; do not sleep with electric pad on

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Soak in warm (not hot) bath for 15–20 minutes; add Epsom salts for muscle relaxation
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Avoid very hot water if prone to fainting or low blood pressure

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: 10–20 minute gentle walk increases endorphins and reduces prostaglandin sensitivity
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Skip if flow is very heavy or fainting risk is present

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Child's Pose, Supine Twist, Cat-Cow – hold each pose 30–60 seconds, repeat 3–5 times
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Avoid inversions during very heavy bleeding

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Butterfly stretch, pigeon pose, hip circles – 10 minutes daily during menstruation
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Stop immediately if pain increases sharply

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Increase omega-3 (salmon, flaxseed), leafy greens, berries; reduce red meat and refined sugar
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Consult GP if on blood thinners due to omega-3 anticoagulant effect

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Dark chocolate 85%+, spinach, almonds, pumpkin seeds; 200–400 mg/day supplement option
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Excess supplementation may cause loose stools; stay at or below 350 mg/day

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: 2–2.5 litres water daily; ginger or chamomile tea reduces prostaglandins and GI cramping
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Avoid excessive caffeine which worsens cramps and anxiety

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Cut sodium to under 1500 mg/day in luteal phase to minimise water retention and bloating
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Essential if taking diuretics – monitor electrolytes closely

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Red meat, lentils, spinach, tofu paired with Vitamin C for better absorption
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Avoid excess iron supplements without a blood test

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Dairy, fortified plant milk, kale – 1200 mg/day may reduce PMS symptoms by up to 48%
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Excess calcium supplements may increase kidney stone risk

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: 400–600 mg every 6–8 hours with food; start 1 day before expected period for best effect
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Avoid with stomach ulcers, kidney disease or blood thinners

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: 500–1000 mg every 4–6 hours (maximum 4 g per day); safer for stomach than NSAIDs
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Avoid alcohol; do not exceed 4 g/day; caution in liver disease

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: 220–440 mg every 8–12 hours; longer-acting NSAID suitable for full-day relief
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Same cautions as ibuprofen; avoid in last trimester of pregnancy

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Reduces uterine spasm; 10–20 mg up to 3 times per day (check local brand availability)
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Can cause dry mouth, blurred vision and urinary retention

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Prioritise consistent sleep schedule; pain is significantly amplified by sleep deprivation
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Avoid screens 1 hour before bed; use eye mask if light is an issue

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Mindfulness 10 min/day, deep breathing (4-7-8 technique), journaling to reduce cortisol
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Seek professional help if anxiety or depression persists beyond period

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation applied to abdomen or back; rent or purchase device
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Avoid over heart, head; do not use during pregnancy; consult if pacemaker present

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Caffeine constricts blood vessels worsening headaches; alcohol increases prostaglandin production
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Gradual reduction recommended to prevent caffeine-withdrawal headache

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Seek medical advice if pain score is 8 or above, fever over 38°C, passing large clots, soaking more than 1 pad per hour, or pain occurs between periods
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Could indicate endometriosis, fibroids, PMOS or pelvic infection

precautions_and_remedies.instructions: Go to emergency immediately for sudden severe pelvic pain, fainting, suspected ectopic pregnancy, or abnormal discharge with high fever
precautions_and_remedies.caution: Do not wait – these are potential medical emergencies

conditions.name: Primary Dysmenorrhoea
conditions.distinguishing_feature: No underlying pathology; caused entirely by prostaglandins
conditions.prognosis: Often improves with age or after childbirth

conditions.name: Secondary Dysmenorrhoea
conditions.distinguishing_feature: Pain caused by an underlying gynaecological condition; tends to worsen over time

conditions.name: Endometriosis
conditions.distinguishing_feature: Endometrial-like tissue grows outside the uterus causing chronic inflammation
conditions.prognosis: Chronic condition; managed but not cured; symptoms can improve with treatment

conditions.name: PMOS (Polyendocrine Metabolic Ovarian Syndrome)
conditions.distinguishing_feature: Multiple ovarian cysts with underlying hormonal imbalance (elevated androgens)

conditions.name: Uterine Fibroids
conditions.distinguishing_feature: Non-cancerous smooth muscle tumours within or on the uterine wall

conditions.name: Adenomyosis
conditions.distinguishing_feature: Endometrial glands embedded within the uterine muscle (myometrium)

conditions.name: PMS (Premenstrual Syndrome)
conditions.distinguishing_feature: Symptoms occur only in luteal phase and fully resolve with period onset
conditions.diagnosis_method: Symptom diary tracked over 2 or more cycles

conditions.name: PMDD (Premenstrual Dysphoric Disorder)
conditions.distinguishing_feature: Severe psychiatric symptoms that significantly impair daily functioning
conditions.diagnosis_method: DSM-5 diagnostic criteria; prospective symptom diary
conditions.prognosis: Treatable; significant improvement with appropriate therapy

conditions.name: Menstrual Anaemia
conditions.distinguishing_feature: Iron depletion caused by heavy or prolonged menstrual blood loss
conditions.prevalence: ~30% of women with heavy menstrual bleeding
conditions.diagnosis_method: Full blood count (FBC) and ferritin blood test

pain_severity_scale.physical_experience: Noticeable dull ache; background awareness throughout the day
pain_severity_scale.functional_impact: Minor distraction; can work and study normally
pain_severity_scale.recommended_action: OTC paracetamol if desired; heat therapy

pain_severity_scale.physical_experience: Persistent cramping or aching; cannot fully ignore
Intense cramping, throbbing or shooting pain
pain_severity_scale.functional_impact: Cannot concentrate; misses work or school; bed rest needed
pain_severity_scale.recommended_action: Prescription-strength NSAIDs; see GP if recurring

pain_severity_scale.physical_experience: Excruciating pain; may cause vomiting, fainting or inability to move
pain_severity_scale.functional_impact: Completely incapacitating; requires immediate medical care
pain_severity_scale.recommended_action: Seek emergency medical attention; rule out serious causes
